name: HEX_PROCESS_METADATA
description: The table stores the metadata for the process being executed.

columns.Name: PROCESS_ID
columns.Comments: The column indicates a sequence generated value.

columns.Name: PROCESS_CODE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Consumer Process Code.

columns.Name: PROCESS_NAME
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Consumer Process Name.

columns.Name: CLASS_NAME
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Consumer Implementation Class Name.

columns.Name: SUB_CLASS_NAME
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Consumer Implementation Sub Class Name.

columns.Name: PRE_PROCESS_PROCEDURE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Consumer Implementation pre process Procedure Name.

columns.Name: PROCESS_PROCEDURE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Consumer Implementation Procedure Name.

columns.Name: POST_PROCESS_PROCEDURE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Consumer Implementation post process Procedure Name.

columns.Name: ACTIVE_STATUS
columns.Comments: The column indicates if the specified process is active.

columns.Name: DEFAULT_THREADS
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Default Thread Count value.

columns.Name: DEFAULT_CHUNK_SIZE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Default Chunk Size value.

columns.Name: DEFAULT_LOGGING
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Default Logging value.

columns.Name: DEFAULT_MAX_ERRORS
columns.Comments: The column indicates the Default Max Errors Allowed value.

columns.Name: ALLOW_OVERRIDE_DEFAULTS
columns.Comments: The column indicates if the default values can be overridden with Payroll Configuration Group.

columns.Name: CONFIG_GROUP_TYPE
columns.Comments: The column indicates the type of configuration group used.

columns.Name: DEFAULT_CONFIG_GROUP
columns.Comments: The column indicates the default configuration group to be used for the process.

columns.Name: RETRIES
columns.Comments: The column indicates the number of retries allowed.

columns.Name: ELEVATED
columns.Comments: The column indicates if the current process is elevated. Valid values are Y and N.Default value is N.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments: Identifier of Enterprise, used for multi-tenancy partitioning. Foreign key to HR_ORGANIZATION_UNITS.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: SGUID
columns.Comments: The seed global unique identifier. Oracle internal use only.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
